```{r paquetes, include=FALSE}
###se  cargan todos los paquetes
library(glmmTMB)
library(performance)
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)
library(itsadug)
library(data.table)
library(moments)
library(tidyverse)
library(readODS)
library(readxl)
library(patchwork)
library(lme4)
library(effects)
library(sjPlot)
library(lmerTest)
library(visreg)
library(car)
library(SPEI)
library(lmtest)
library(multcompView)
library(cowplot)
library(merTools)
library(plyr)
library(gamm4)
library(DHARMa)
library(MuMIn) ###para seleccionar modelo
source("C:/Minicores/R_analisis/Analisis_anatomia/Anatomy_function.R") ##en windows
```

```{r datos, include=FALSE}
###clima
clima <- list()

clima$prp <- read_csv("C:/Minicores/HOBO/precipitaciones/Gamirprcp2019-2022.csv")

setwd("C:/Minicores/HOBO/Temperatura")
Temp_gamir_Nov20_Feb21 <- read_excel("Gamir_Nov20_Feb21.xlsx")
Temp_gamir_Feb21_Jun21 <- read_excel("Gamir_Feb21_Jun21.xlsx")
Temp_gamir_Jun21_May22 <- read_excel("Gamir_Jun21_May22.xlsx")
###le doy el formato de fecha
Temp_gamir_Feb21_Jun21$date <- as.Date( Temp_gamir_Feb21_Jun21$`Fecha/hora (CET)`, "%m/%d/%y")
Temp_gamir_Jun21_May22$date <- as.Date( Temp_gamir_Jun21_May22$`Fecha/hora (CET)`, "%y/%m/%d")
###me quedo con la humedad relativa y la temperatura
Temp_gamir_Feb21_May22 <- rbind(Temp_gamir_Feb21_Jun21,Temp_gamir_Jun21_May22)  
Temp_gamir_Feb21_May22 <- Temp_gamir_Feb21_May22[names(Temp_gamir_Feb21_May22) %in% c("date" ,"Temperatura_(C)", "Humedad_relativa")]

####le hago la media de temperatura al df Temp_gamir_Feb21_Jun21
Temp_gamir_Nov20_Feb21$Tmean <- rowMeans(Temp_gamir_Nov20_Feb21[, c("Tmin","Tmax")] , na.rm = T)
Temp_gamir_Nov20_Feb21$date <- as.Date(Temp_gamir_Nov20_Feb21$date)


clima$temp <- Temp_gamir_Feb21_May22
clima$temp_Feb21  <- Temp_gamir_Nov20_Feb21

#Humedad suelo
clima$Hum <- read_delim("C:/Minicores/HOBO/Humedad_suelo/Humedad_suelo_Modificado.csv", delim = ";" )


rm(Temp_gamir_Nov20_Feb21, Temp_gamir_Feb21_Jun21, Temp_gamir_Jun21_May22, Temp_gamir_Feb21_May22 )

###EW/LW/TRW/Area
Minicore_fielddata <- list()
setwd("C:/Minicores/R_analisis")
Minicore_fielddata$Minicore_TRW <- read_excel_allsheets("Datos_Modificados_Qca.xlsx")
Minicore_fielddata$Datos_pie <- read_ods("Datos_Pie.ods")
Minicore_fielddata$Datos_pie <- split(Minicore_fielddata$Datos_pie, Minicore_fielddata$Datos_pie$Especie)
Minicore_fielddata$Datos_pie <- Minicore_fielddata$Datos_pie$QUCA


```

```{r clima, include=FALSE}
###creo una lista para meter los gráficos
clima_plot <- list()

#######Temperatura y VPD

temperatura <- clima$temp 

##calculo el SPD y el AVP
temperatura$SVP <- 0.6108 * exp((17.27 * temperatura$`Temperatura_(C)`) / (temperatura$`Temperatura_(C)` + 237.3))
temperatura$AVP <-  temperatura$SVP * (temperatura$Humedad_relativa / 100)
temperatura$VPD <-  temperatura$SVP - temperatura$AVP

##hago la media diaria de los valores de temperatura, humedad, maximo y minimo de temperatura diaria
temperatura <- split(temperatura, temperatura$date)
temperatura <- lapply(temperatura, function(x){
  df <-  data.frame("Tmean" = mean(x$`Temperatura_(C)`), "Tmin" = min(x$`Temperatura_(C)`), "Tmax" = max(x$`Temperatura_(C)`), "VPD" = mean(x$VPD) )
})
x <- 0
while(x < length(temperatura)){
  x <- x +1
  temperatura[[x]]$date <- names(temperatura[x])
}
rm(x)
temperatura <- do.call(rbind, Map(data.frame,temperatura ) )

temperatura$date <- as.Date(temperatura$date)

clima$VPD <- data.frame("date" = temperatura$date, "VPD" = temperatura$VPD)
temperatura$VPD <- NULL 

##ahora uno ambas temperaturas

clima$temp <- rbind(clima$temp_Feb21 ,temperatura)
clima$temp_Feb21 <- NULL

###sectorizo  las fechas tanto de prp como de temperatura


clima <- lapply(clima, function(x){
  ifelse("date" %in% names(x), x <-  x[x$date >= as.Date("2020-11-01") & x$date <= as.Date("2022-02-28"),], x <- x )  
              return(x)})

clima$prp$date <- as.Date(clima$prp$date)
precipitacion <- clima$prp
precipitacion <- precipitacion[names(precipitacion) %in% c("date","Prcp")]

precipitacion$year_month <- format(precipitacion$date, "%Y-%m")

group_month <- aggregate(Prcp ~ year_month, data = precipitacion, sum)
group_month$date <- as.Date(paste(group_month$year_month,'-01',sep = "")) 


###total de precipitacion
sum(precipitacion$Prcp)


##grafico precipitacion
precipitacion$doy <- yday(precipitacion$date)

clima_plot$prp <- ggplot()+
  geom_bar(data=precipitacion, aes(date, as.numeric(Prcp)),stat = "identity",
           show.legend = FALSE,position = "dodge", fill= "#00BFFF")+
  theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
          axis.line.x = element_line(colour="black"),
          axis.line.y = element_line(colour="black"))+
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 month" ,date_labels = " ")+
  ylab("precipitation (mm)")+
  xlab(" ")





###figura 2

temperatura <- clima$temp

clima_plot$temp <-  ggplot() + geom_line(data=temperatura, aes(as.Date(date, "%y/%m/%d"), Tmean), color = "black", lwd= 0.9)+
geom_line(data=temperatura, aes(as.Date(date, "%y/%m/%d"), Tmax), color = "#FF3030", lwd= 0.9)+
  geom_line(data=temperatura, aes(as.Date(date, "%y/%m/%d"), Tmin), color = "#1C86EE", lwd= 0.9)+
  scale_y_continuous(name="Temperature (ºC)",breaks= c(-5,0,5,10,15,20,25,30,35,40),
                     expand = c(0,0))+
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 month" ,date_labels = " ")+
  theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
          axis.line.x = element_line(colour="black"),
          axis.line.y = element_line(colour="black"))+
  xlab(" ")


####Humedad suelo

Hum_suelo <- clima$Hum

Hum_suelo <- split(Hum_suelo, Hum_suelo$Site)
Hum_suelo <- Hum_suelo$Gamir
nombre_columnas_fecha <- names(Hum_suelo)  ##cojo los nombres de la humedad de suelo (las fechas)
nombre_columnas_fecha <- nombre_columnas_fecha[!(nombre_columnas_fecha %in% c("Site", "Plot","Point","Treatment","Depth")) ]#elimino las variables que no contemplo para este plot

Hum_suelo <- reshape2::melt(Hum_suelo, id.vars=c("Site", "Plot","Point","Treatment","Depth"), 
                     measure.vars= nombre_columnas_fecha, 
                     value.name="values", 
                     variable.name="date")

##veo como se comportan las variables

Hum_suelo$site_tr <- paste0(Hum_suelo$Site, "_", Hum_suelo$Treatment)
ggplot(data= Hum_suelo, aes(x= as.Date(date, format = "%d/%m/%Y"), y= as.numeric(values), color= site_tr))+
  geom_point()+
  facet_grid(. ~ Depth)+
  geom_smooth()+
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,60))


Hum_suelo$date <- as.Date(Hum_suelo$date, format = "%d/%m/%Y")
Hum_suelo$date1 <- as.numeric(Hum_suelo$date)
Hum_suelo$values <- as.numeric(Hum_suelo$values)
Hum_suelo$Depth <- as.factor(Hum_suelo$Depth)
Hum_suelo$Plot <- as.factor(Hum_suelo$Plot)
Hum_suelo$Treatment <- as.factor(Hum_suelo$Treatment)
Hum_suelo$Depth_point <- paste0(Hum_suelo$Point, "_", Hum_suelo$Depth)


Hum_suelo[duplicated(Hum_suelo[, c("Plot", "Point", "date1")]), ]

modelo_humedad <- gamm4(values ~ Treatment * Depth + s(date1, k = 10),
     random = ~(1|Plot/Point),
     data = Hum_suelo)

###duda si es el random Point o el Point_depth


summary(modelo_humedad$gam)

residuos <- residuals(modelo_humedad$mer)
qqnorm(residuos)
qqline(residuos)
hist(residuos) ###son normales
###preguntar heterosasticidad

###le hago el predict al modelo
predict_Humedad <- get_predictions(modelo_humedad$gam, 
                       cond = list( Depth = rep( unique(Hum_suelo$Depth), length(unique(Hum_suelo$date1))),
                                    date1= rep(unique(Hum_suelo$date1), each=8),
                                    Treatment= rep(unique(Hum_suelo$Treatment), each=4),
                                    Plot = 1,
                                    Point = 1, se= T))


predict_Humedad <- unique(predict_Humedad)

clima_plot$Hum <-  ggplot()+
  geom_point(data = Hum_suelo, aes(x= as.Date(Hum_suelo$date1), y= as.numeric(values), colour= Treatment), alpha= 0.1)+
  geom_line(data= predict_Humedad, aes(x= as.Date(date1), y= fit, colour = Treatment), lwd=0.9, show.legend = F)+
  facet_grid(. ~ Depth)+
  theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
          axis.line.x = element_line(colour="black"),
          axis.line.y = element_line(colour="black"))+
  scale_color_manual(values=c("#0000FF",'#CD3333'))+
  geom_ribbon(data= predict_Humedad, alpha=.3, mapping = aes(x=as.Date(date1), ymin= fit-CI, ymax= fit +CI, fill=Treatment),colour= NA, show.legend = F)+
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#00EEEE",'#FF4040'))


###SPEI
 

clima$temp$PET <- hargreaves(Tmin=clima$temp$Tmin, Tmax=clima$temp$Tmax, lat= 36.569 )

clima$temp$prp <- clima$prp$Prcp

Prp_PET <- clima$temp$prp - clima$temp$PET

clima$SPEI <- spei(Prp_PET, scale =1 )

clima$SPEI <- data.frame(
  time = seq.Date(from = as.Date("2020-11-01"), by = "day", length.out = length(clima$SPEI$fitted)),
  spei = clima$SPEI$fitted
)


clima$SPEI$sing <- ifelse(as.numeric(clima$SPEI$spei) >= 0, "pos", "neg") 

clima_plot$SPEI <-  ggplot(clima$SPEI, aes(x = time, y = spei, col = sing, fill = sing)) +
  geom_bar(show.legend = F, stat = "identity") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("pos" = "darkblue", "neg" = "red")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("pos"  = "darkblue", "neg" = "red")) +
  labs( x= " ", y = "SPEI") +
  theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
          axis.line.x = element_line(colour="black"),
          axis.line.y = element_line(colour="black"))+
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 month" ,date_labels = "%m")

##VPD

clima_plot$prp <- ggplot()+
  geom_line(data = clima$VPD, aes(x=date, y=VPD*10), color= "black", lwd=0.7, linetype= 8)+
  geom_bar(data=clima$prp, aes(date, as.numeric(Prcp)),stat = "identity",
           show.legend = FALSE,position = "dodge", fill= "#00BFFF")+
  theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
          axis.line.x = element_line(colour="black"),
          axis.line.y.left = element_line(colour="black"),
        axis.line.y.right = element_line(colour="black" ))+
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 month" ,date_labels = " ")+
  scale_y_continuous(sec.axis = sec_axis(~(./10),name="VPD (Kpa)",breaks= c(1,2,3,4)),
                     name = "precipitation (mm)",breaks = c(0,10,20,30,40), expand = c(0,0))+
  xlab(" ") 

##Plots


plot_grid(clima_plot$temp,clima_plot$prp,clima_plot$SPEI, ncol = 1)


```

```{r Phenology canariensis_EW, include=FALSE}
Qca_phenology <- Minicore_fielddata$Minicore_TRW
Qca_phenology <- Qca_phenology[ names(Qca_phenology) != "ray_area"]
Qca_phenology <- eliminar_filas_NA_lista(Qca_phenology, "N_vasos")


 ###elimino las columnas y las fechas que no son interesantes para este analisis
Qca_phenology <- lapply(Qca_phenology, function(x){
  if( !(("Inicio_EW" %in% names(x)) | ("Inicio_LW" %in% names(x)))) {return(NULL)}
  return(x)
})
Qca_phenology <- Qca_phenology[!sapply(Qca_phenology, is.null)]

Qca_phenology <- lapply(Qca_phenology, function(x) x[(names(x) %in% c("Plot", "Tratam", "indv","Inicio_EW", "Inicio_LW"))])

##añadimos una columna con las fechas del muestreo 
fechas <- c("02/02/2021", "18/03/2021","01/04/2021","15/04/2021","29/04/2021","13/05/2021","27/05/2021" ,"10/06/2021","22/07/2021","10/08/2021")
Qca_phenology <- mapply(cbind, Qca_phenology, "date"=as.Date(fechas, "%d/%m/%Y" ), SIMPLIFY=F)


####junto datos de pie a individuo
Minicore_fielddata$Datos_pie <- Minicore_fielddata$Datos_pie[!names(Minicore_fielddata$Datos_pie) %in% c("Especie", "Finca","Tratam","Plot")]
names(Minicore_fielddata$Datos_pie)[names(Minicore_fielddata$Datos_pie) == 'Árbol'] <- 'indv'

Qca_phenology <- lapply(Qca_phenology, function(x) {x <- merge(x,Minicore_fielddata$Datos_pie, by=c("indv"))
 if(!( "Inicio_LW" %in% names(x))){x$Inicio_LW <- NA}
 if(!("Inicio_EW" %in% names(x))){x$Inicio_EW <- NA}
return(x)
})

Qca_phenology <- do.call(rbind, Map(data.frame, Qca_phenology))

####empezamos a hacer el analisis de EW y LW

Qca_phenology$Inicio_EW <- as.factor(tolower(Qca_phenology$Inicio_EW))
Qca_phenology$Inicio_LW <- as.factor(tolower(Qca_phenology$Inicio_LW))
Qca_phenology$date_1 <- yday(Qca_phenology$date) ##se reformula la fecha en DOY
Qca_phenology$Tratam <- as.factor(Qca_phenology$Tratam)
Qca_phenology$Plot <- as.factor(Qca_phenology$Plot)
Qca_phenology$indv <- as.factor(Qca_phenology$indv)
Qca_phenology$Inicio_EW_bin <- ifelse(Qca_phenology$Inicio_EW == "si", 1, 0)
Qca_phenology$Inicio_LW_bin <- ifelse(Qca_phenology$Inicio_LW == "si", 1, 0)
####veo si hay colinealidad en mis variables
df <- data.frame("DBH"= Qca_phenology$DBH_.cm. , "H"= Qca_phenology$H_.cm. , "Dcopa"= Qca_phenology$Diametro_copa_.cm. )
cor(df)
summary(lm(formula = DBH ~ ., data = df)) #####correlacionado el H con el DBH, por lo tanto elijo la altura como variables
rm(df)

##modelos ########mirar con ELI pongo solo Plot ya que PLOT recoge casi toda la variabilidad
modelo1 <- glm(data = Qca_phenology, Inicio_EW_bin ~ scale(date_1) + scale(H_.cm.) + scale(Diametro_copa_.cm.) + Tratam  , family = binomial) ##29.0
summary(modelo1)$varcor
modelo2 <- glmer(data = Qca_phenology, Inicio_EW_bin ~ scale(date_1) + scale(Diametro_copa_.cm.)+ Tratam  + (1|indv), family = binomial) ##36.9
modelo3 <- glmer(data = Qca_phenology, Inicio_EW_bin ~ scale(date_1) + scale(H_.cm.)+ Tratam  + (1|indv), family = binomial) ##36.8
# modelo4 <- glmer(data = Qca_phenology, Inicio_EW_bin ~ scale(date_1) + scale(H_.cm.)*scale(Diametro_copa_.cm.)+ Tratam  + (1|indv), family = binomial) ##35.4 ##me genera error


modelo5 <- glmer(data = Qca_phenology, Inicio_EW_bin ~ scale(date_1)+ Tratam  + (1|indv), family = binomial) #30.2



model.sel(modelo1, modelo2, modelo3, modelo5) ###mejor modelo es el modelo 1


r2(modelo5)
summary(modelo1)
residuos <- residuals(modelo5)
qqnorm(residuos)
qqline(residuos)
hist(residuos) ###son normales

#####graficarlo

df <- expand.grid(
  Tratam = levels(Qca_phenology$Tratam),
  H_.cm. = mean(Qca_phenology$H_.cm., na.rm=TRUE),
  Diametro_copa_.cm. = mean(Qca_phenology$Diametro_copa_.cm., na.rm=TRUE),
  date_1 = seq(min(Qca_phenology$date_1), max(Qca_phenology$date_1), length.out = 100),
  indv= 5,
  Plot = 1
)


predict_Ci <- predictInterval(modelo5,
                              newdata = df,
                              level = 0.95,
                              type = "probability",
                              stat = "mean")

df$fit <- predict_Ci$fit
df$upr <- predict_Ci$upr
df$lwr <- predict_Ci$lwr
ggplot(data = df, aes(x = date_1, y=fit, colour = Tratam ))+
  geom_line()+
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lwr, ymax = upr, fill = Tratam), alpha = 0.2, color = NA)+
  scale_color_manual(values= c("blue", "red"))+
  scale_fill_manual(values= c("blue", "red"))+
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(30,150), breaks = seq(from=40, to=160, by=20))


# Día donde prob supera 0.5 = inicio estimado de EW
# inicio_est <- newdat$date_1[which.min(abs(newdat$prob_EW - 0.5))]
# inicio_est


```

```{r Phenology canariensis_LW, include=FALSE}
rm(modelo1, modelo2, modelo3, modelo4, modelo5)
##modelos
modelo1 <- glmer(data = Qca_phenology, Inicio_LW_bin ~ scale(date_1) + scale(H_.cm.) + scale(Diametro_copa_.cm.) + Tratam  + (1|indv), family = binomial) ##56.3
summary(modelo1)$varcor #####no es por el random factor
########me da error con el factor de Plot no explica mi varianza
modelo2 <- glmer(data = Qca_phenology, Inicio_LW_bin ~ scale(date_1) + scale(Diametro_copa_.cm.)+ Tratam  + (1|indv), family = binomial) ##54.3
modelo3 <- glmer(data = Qca_phenology, Inicio_LW_bin ~ scale(date_1) + scale(H_.cm.)+ Tratam  + (1|indv), family = binomial) ##53.9
modelo4 <- glmer(data = Qca_phenology, Inicio_LW_bin ~ scale(date_1) + scale(H_.cm.)*scale(Diametro_copa_.cm.)+ Tratam  + (1|indv), family = binomial) ##58.7
modelo5 <- glmer(data = Qca_phenology, Inicio_LW_bin ~ scale(date_1)+ Tratam  + (1|indv), family = binomial) #52.7



model.sel(modelo1, modelo2, modelo3, modelo4, modelo5) ###mejor modelo es el modelo 5


r2(modelo4)
summary(modelo4)
residuos <- residuals(modelo4)
qqnorm(residuos)
qqline(residuos)
hist(residuos) ###son normales

#####graficarlo

df$indv <- 2
predict_Ci <- predictInterval(modelo4,
                              newdata = df,
                              level = 0.95,
                              type = "probability",
                              stat = "mean")

df$fit <- predict_Ci$fit
df$upr <- predict_Ci$upr
df$lwr <- predict_Ci$lwr
ggplot(data = df, aes(x = date_1, y=fit, colour = Tratam ))+
  geom_line()+
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lwr, ymax = upr, fill = Tratam), alpha = 0.2, color = NA)+
  scale_color_manual(values= c("blue", "red"))+
  scale_fill_manual(values= c("blue", "red"))


# Día donde prob supera 0.5 = inicio estimado de EW
# inicio_est <- newdat$date_1[which.min(abs(newdat$prob_EW - 0.5))]
# inicio_est


######el modelo da issingular hay que arreglarlo

rm(modelo1, modelo2, modelo3, modelo4, modelo5)
```

```{r Análisis de 2020/2021 Qca, include=FALSE}
##2020 EW
setwd("C:/Minicores/Qca_Bruto/ImageJ/Tablas/a4-02-2021")
name_documents <- list.files(pattern = '*.csv') ##hace una lista con todos los nombres de archivos csv de la carpeta
Qca2020 <- lapply(name_documents, read_delim, delim = ',') #se abren los documentos seleccionados
names(Qca2020) <- name_documents #conecta los nombre de la lista con los nombres de los documentos

EW_2020 <- list("Qc2.20" = Qca2020$a2QcC4F21.csv[c(1,20), ],"Qc3.20" = Qca2020$b3QcC_4F21.csv[c(32,44, 48,49), ], "Qc5.20" =Qca2020$c5QcC4F21.csv[c(10,21,23,32,43,57,64), ], "Qc8.20" = Qca2020$d8QcC4F21.csv[c(1,3,9,18), ],"Qc9.20" = Qca2020$e9QcC4F21.csv[c(19), ],"Qc10.20" = Qca2020$f10QcC4F21.csv[c(7,18), ],"Qc18.20" = Qca2020$g18QcS4F21.csv[c(8), ],"Qc19.20" = Qca2020$h19QcS4F21.csv[c(1,3), ],"Qc26.20" = Qca2020$i26QcS4F21.csv[c(1,19,30,31), ],"Qc30.20" = Qca2020$j30QcCSF21.csv[c(14,30,42, 51,57), ], "Qc40.20" = Qca2020$k40QcC4F21.csv[c(1,8,27), ], "Qc44.20" = Qca2020$l44QcC4F21.csv[c(7,12,20,26,29), ],"Qc47.20" = Qca2020$m47QcS4F21.csv[c(1,2,12,13,42,43), ],"Qc52.20" = Qca2020$n52QcS4F21.csv[c(4,6,25,27,31,33,37,38,44), ]) #####se hace una lista con todos los vasos de EW del año 2020

###Separamos entre control y sequia
x <- 0
while (x < length(EW_2020)) {
  x <- x+1
  nombre <- names(EW_2020[x])
  EW_2020[[x]]$indv <- nombre
  if((names(EW_2020[x]) == "Qc18.20") | (names(EW_2020[x]) == "Qc19.20") | (names(EW_2020[x]) == "Qc26.20") | (names(EW_2020[x]) == "Qc47.20") | (names(EW_2020[x]) == "Qc52.20")){EW_2020[[x]]$treatment <- "drought" }else{
    EW_2020[[x]]$treatment <- "control"}
}
rm(x)

EW_2020 <- lapply(EW_2020, function(x){x[c("indv", 'Area', 'Feret', "treatment")]})

EW_2020_df <- do.call(rbind.data.frame, EW_2020)

ggplot(data =EW_2020_df, aes(x= treatment, y= Area))+
  geom_violin()+
  geom_point()

anova_area <- aov(data =EW_2020_df, Area~ treatment )
summary(anova_area)

EW_vessel_treatment <- split(EW_2020_df, EW_2020_df$treatment)

##2021 EW
setwd("C:/Minicores/Qca_Bruto/ImageJ/Tablas/l19-1-2022")
name_documents <- list.files(pattern = '*.csv') ##hace una lista con todos los nombres de archivos csv de la carpeta
Qca2021 <- lapply(name_documents, read_delim, delim = ',') #se abren los documentos seleccionados
names(Qca2021) <- name_documents #conecta los nombre de la lista con los nombres de los documentos

EW_2021 <- list("Qc2.21" = Qca2021$a2QcC19E22.csv[c(1,9,11,12,14,16,17,24,28,29,31,35,38,43,45), ],"Qc3.21" = Qca2021$b3QcC19E22.csv[c(1,29,30,35,55,58,59), ], "Qc5.21" =Qca2021$c5QcC19E22.csv[c(1,6,13,15,19,24,32,37,39,40), ], "Qc9.21" = Qca2021$d9QcC19E22.csv[c(18,27), ],"Qc18.21" = Qca2021$h18QcS19E22.csv[c(19,21,23,32,36), ],"Qc19.21" = Qca2021$i19QcS19E22.csv[c(1,23,36), ],"Qc30.21" = Qca2021$j30QcS19E22.csv[c(2,9,16,21,25), ], "Qc36.21" = Qca2021$k36QcC19E22.csv[c(1,6), ], "Qc40.21" = Qca2021$m40QcC19E22.csv[c(1,16,29), ], "Qc44.21" = Qca2021$n44QcC19E22.csv[c(5,10,19,42), ],"Qc47.21" = Qca2021$o47QcS19E22.csv[c(1,3,4), ],"Qc52.21" = Qca2021$r52QcS19E22.csv[c(7,13,21,29), ]) #####se hace una lista con todos los vasos de EW del año 2021


x <- 0
while (x < length(EW_2021)) {
  x <- x+1
  nombre <- names(EW_2021[x])
  EW_2021[[x]]$indv <- nombre
  if((names(EW_2021[x]) == "Qc18.21") | (names(EW_2021[x]) == "Qc19.21") | (names(EW_2021[x]) == "Qc26.21") | (names(EW_2021[x]) == "Qc47.21") | (names(EW_2021[x]) == "Qc52.21")){EW_2021[[x]]$treatment <- "drought" }else{
    EW_2021[[x]]$treatment <- "control"}
}
rm(x)

EW_2021 <- lapply(EW_2021, function(x){x[c("indv", 'Area', 'Feret', "treatment")]})

EW_2021_df <- do.call(rbind.data.frame, EW_2021)

ggplot(data =EW_2021_df, aes(x= treatment, y= as.numeric(Area)))+
  geom_violin()

anova_area <- aov(data =EW_2021_df, Area~ treatment )
summary(anova_area)

##junto los datos del 2020 y 2021

EW20.21 <- rbind(EW_2020_df, EW_2021_df)

EW20.21$Area_Feret <- 0.785*(EW20.21$Feret^2)
ggplot(data =EW20.21, aes(x= treatment, y= Area_Feret))+
  geom_boxplot()

#######preguntar a lorena #######################
qqnorm(EW20.21$Area)
qqline(EW20.21$Area)
shapiro.test(EW20.21$Area) ###no es normal
hist(EW20.21$Area)


skewness(EW20.21$Area, na.rm = TRUE) #veo que dispersion tiene los datos
EW20.21$trans <- log10(EW20.21$Area) ##realizo la transformacion
qqnorm(EW20.21$trans)
qqline(EW20.21$trans)
shapiro.test(EW20.21$trans) ###veo que mis datos ya son normales
hist(EW20.21$trans)

anova_area <- aov(data =EW20.21, Area~ treatment )
summary(anova_area)

aggregate(Area~ treatment,EW20.21, mean )
aggregate(Area~ treatment,EW20.21, se )


###numero de EW vessel por indv en el area
Freqency_vessel <- as.data.frame(table(EW20.21$indv))
Freqency_vessel$treatment <- ifelse(grepl( "Qc18", Freqency_vessel$Var1) | grepl( "Qc19", Freqency_vessel$Var1) | grepl( "Qc26", Freqency_vessel$Var1) | grepl( "Qc47", Freqency_vessel$Var1) | grepl( "Qc52", Freqency_vessel$Var1), "drought", "control" )

ggplot(data =Freqency_vessel, aes(x= treatment, y= Freq))+
  geom_violin()+
  geom_point()

qqnorm(Freqency_vessel$Freq)
qqline(Freqency_vessel$Freq)
shapiro.test(Freqency_vessel$Freq) ###veo que mis datos no son normales


skewness(Freqency_vessel$Freq, na.rm = TRUE) #veo que dispersion tiene los datos
Freqency_vessel$trans <- log10(Freqency_vessel$Freq) ##realizo la transformacion
qqnorm(Freqency_vessel$trans)
qqline(Freqency_vessel$trans)
shapiro.test(Freqency_vessel$trans) ###veo que mis datos ya son normales
hist(Freqency_vessel$trans)


anova_freqvessel <- aov(data =Freqency_vessel, trans~ treatment )
summary(anova_freqvessel)

aggregate(Freq~ treatment,Freqency_vessel, mean )
aggregate(Freq~ treatment,Freqency_vessel, se )

##LW2020
LW_2020 <- list("Qc2.20" = Qca2020$a2QcC4F21.csv[-c(1,2,3,5,7,20,8,13,19,10,25,30,36,39), ],"Qc3.20" = Qca2020$b3QcC_4F21.csv[-c(32,44, 48,49,20,42,34,14,41,43,35), ], "Qc5.20" =Qca2020$c5QcC4F21.csv[-c(10,21,23,32,43,57,64,57,62,60,38,50,53,20,25,35,54,63,58,52), ], "Qc8.20" = Qca2020$d8QcC4F21.csv[-c(1,3,9,18), ],"Qc9.20" = Qca2020$e9QcC4F21.csv[-c(4,7,19,33,38,48,51,55,54,56,20,14,8,10,12,16,25,20), ],"Qc10.20" = Qca2020$f10QcC4F21.csv[-c(7,18), ],"Qc18.20" = Qca2020$g18QcS4F21.csv[-c(8), ],"Qc19.20" = Qca2020$h19QcS4F21.csv[-c(1,3), ],"Qc26.20" = Qca2020$i26QcS4F21.csv[-c(1,19,30,31), ],"Qc30.20" = Qca2020$j30QcCSF21.csv[-c(14,30,42, 51,57,20,34,40,52,49,53,56,58,41,38), ], "Qc40.20" = Qca2020$k40QcC4F21.csv[-c(1,8,27), ], "Qc44.20" = Qca2020$l44QcC4F21.csv[-c(7,12,20,26,29), ],"Qc47.20" = Qca2020$m47QcS4F21.csv[-c(1,2,12,13,42,43), ],"Qc52.20" = Qca2020$n52QcS4F21.csv[-c(4,6,25,27,31,33,37,38,44), ]) #####se hace una lista con todos los vasos menos los de EW del año 2020


x <- 0
while (x < length(LW_2020)) {
  x <- x+1
  nombre <- names(LW_2020[x])
  LW_2020[[x]]$indv <- nombre
  if((names(LW_2020[x]) == "Qc18.20") | (names(LW_2020[x]) == "Qc19.20") | (names(LW_2020[x]) == "Qc26.20") | (names(LW_2020[x]) == "Qc47.20") | (names(LW_2020[x]) == "Qc52.20")){LW_2020[[x]]$treatment <- "drought" }else{
    LW_2020[[x]]$treatment <- "control"}
}
rm(x)

LW_2020 <- do.call(rbind.data.frame, LW_2020)
ggplot(LW_2020, aes(x= LW_2020$treatment, y=LW_2020$Area))+geom_boxplot()
##LW2021

LW_2021 <- list("Qc2.21" = Qca2021$a2QcC19E22.csv[-c(1,9,11,12,14,16,17,24,28,29,31,35,38,43,45), ],"Qc3.21" = Qca2021$b3QcC19E22.csv[-c(1,18,19,23,29,30,35,40,46,49,51,53,55,58,59,20,24,2,5,28,42,32), ], "Qc5.21" =Qca2021$c5QcC19E22.csv[-c(1,6,13,15,19,24,32,37,39,40), ], "Qc9.21" = Qca2021$d9QcC19E22.csv[-c(18,27), ],"Qc10.21" = Qca2021$e10QcC19E22.csv[-c(12,22,26,27,33), ],"Qc18.21" = Qca2021$h18QcS19E22.csv[-c(19,21,23,32,36), ],"Qc19.21" = Qca2021$i19QcS19E22.csv[-c(1,23,36), ],"Qc30.21" = Qca2021$j30QcS19E22.csv[-c(2,9,16,21,25), ], "Qc36.21" = Qca2021$k36QcC19E22.csv[-c(1,6), ], "Qc40.21" = Qca2021$m40QcC19E22.csv[-c(1,16,29), ], "Qc44.21" = Qca2021$n44QcC19E22.csv[-c(5,8,10,19,33,38,42,21,13,26,40,46), ],"Qc47.21" = Qca2021$o47QcS19E22.csv[-c(1,3,4), ],"Qc52.21" = Qca2021$r52QcS19E22.csv[-c(7,13,21,29), ]) #####se hace una lista con todos los vasos de EW del año 2021, ademas elimino los fallos que tiene en la lectura de algunos vasos de la LW


x <- 0
while (x < length(LW_2021)) {
  x <- x+1
  nombre <- names(LW_2021[x])
  LW_2021[[x]]$indv <- nombre
  if((names(LW_2021[x]) == "Qc18.21") | (names(LW_2021[x]) == "Qc19.21") | (names(LW_2021[x]) == "Qc26.21") | (names(LW_2021[x]) == "Qc47.21") | (names(LW_2021[x]) == "Qc52.21")){LW_2021[[x]]$treatment <- "drought" }else{
    LW_2021[[x]]$treatment <- "control"}
}
rm(x)

LW_2021 <- lapply(LW_2021, function(x){x[c("indv", 'Area', 'Feret', "treatment")]})

LW_2021 <- do.call(rbind.data.frame, LW_2021)
LW_2021$Area <- ifelse(LW_2021$Area > 0.8, NA, LW_2021$Area)
LW_2021 <- LW_2021[complete.cases(LW_2021), ]
ggplot(LW_2021, aes(x= as.factor(LW_2021$treatment), y= as.numeric(LW_2021$Area)))+geom_boxplot()



##junto los datos del 2020 y 2021

LW20.21 <- rbind(LW_2020, LW_2021)
LW20.21$Area_Feret <- 0.785*(LW20.21$Feret^2)
LW20.21$Area_Feret <- ifelse(LW20.21$Area_Feret > 100, NA, LW20.21$Area_Feret)
ggplot(data =LW20.21, aes(x= treatment, y= Area_Feret))+
  geom_boxplot()

qqnorm(LW20.21$Area_Feret)
qqline(LW20.21$Area_Feret)
shapiro.test(LW20.21$Area_Feret) ###no es normal



anova_area <- kruskal.test(data =LW20.21, Area_Feret~ treatment )
anova_area

aggregate(Area_Feret~ treatment,LW20.21, mean )
aggregate(Area_Feret~ treatment,LW20.21, se )


###numero de LW vessel por indv en el area
Freqency_vessel <- as.data.frame(table(LW20.21$indv))
Freqency_vessel$treatment <- ifelse(grepl( "Qc18", Freqency_vessel$Var1) | grepl( "Qc19", Freqency_vessel$Var1) |  grepl( "Qc47", Freqency_vessel$Var1) | grepl( "Qc52", Freqency_vessel$Var1), "drought", "control" )

ggplot(data =Freqency_vessel, aes(x= treatment, y= Freq))+
  geom_violin()

aggregate(Freq~ treatment,Freqency_vessel, mean )
aggregate(Freq~ treatment,Freqency_vessel, se )
qqnorm(Freqency_vessel$Freq)
qqline(Freqency_vessel$Freq)
shapiro.test(Freqency_vessel$Freq)


anova_freqvessel <- aov(data =Freqency_vessel, Freq~ treatment )
summary(anova_freqvessel)



###TRW
TRW_2020 <- read_excel("C:/Minicores/R_analisis/Datos_Modificados_Qca.xlsx", 
    sheet = "2Feb21")
TRW_2021 <- read_excel("C:/Minicores/R_analisis/Datos_Modificados_Qca.xlsx", 
    sheet = "19Ene22")

TRW20.21 <- list(TRW_2020, TRW_2021)

TRW20.21 <- lapply(TRW20.21, function(x){
   df <- x[!(is.na(x$N_vasos) |x$N_vasos == " ") ,  ] ###elimino las filas que no me afectan para este calculo
 
 df <- df[, ! names(df) %in% c("N_anillos","N_vasos","Feret", "Area", "Inicio_EW", "Finca", "escala 1 microm"), drop = F]
 df$TRW_mean  <-  rowMeans(df[ ,names(df) %in% c("TRW (mm) arriba", "TRW (mm) centro", "TRW (mm) Abajo")], na.rm = T)
 return(df)
})

TRW_2020 <- as.data.frame(TRW20.21[1])
TRW_2021 <- as.data.frame(TRW20.21[2])

ggplot(data = TRW_2021, aes(x= Tratam, y= TRW_mean ))+
  geom_boxplot()

shapiro.test(TRW_2020$TRW_mean) ##son normales
shapiro.test(TRW_2021$TRW_mean) ##son normales
leveneTest(y =TRW_2020$TRW_mean, group =TRW_2020$Tratam, center = "median") ###no hay evidencias significativas de que las varianzas sean diferentes , se cumple el supuesto de homogeneidad

###EW vulnerability index
EW_2020_feret <-lapply(EW_2020, function(x){  Feret_converter(x, "mm")}) ##primero convierto cada vaso en las variables relacionadas con Feret
EW_2021_feret <-lapply(EW_2021, function(x){  Feret_converter(x, "mm")})

Data_2020 <- read_excel("C:/Minicores/R_analisis/Datos_Modificados_Qca.xlsx", 
    sheet = "2Feb21")
Data_2021 <- read_excel("C:/Minicores/R_analisis/Datos_Modificados_Qca.xlsx", 
     sheet = "19Ene22")

EW_20.21_feret <- list("EW_2020_feret" =EW_2020_feret,"EW_2021_feret" = EW_2021_feret) ##junto los datos
Data_20.21 <- list("Data_2020"= Data_2020, "Data_2021" = Data_2021)

Data_20.21 <- lapply(Data_20.21, function(x){
  df <- x[!(is.na(x$Area) |x$Area == " ") ,  ] 
  df <- df[ ,c("indv", "Area")]
  names(df)[2] <- "year_Total_area"
return(df)
}) ##modifico los datos para que contengan las mismas columnas


EW_20.21_feret <- lapply(EW_20.21_feret, function(x){
  z <- 0
while(z < length(x)){
  z <- z +1
  x[[z]]$indv <- names(x[z])
} ###hace que nos cree la columna de individuos

df <- bind_rows(x) ###concentro todos los datos en un data frame

df[c("1", "2")] <-  str_split_fixed(df$indv, ".20" , 2)
df[c("1", "indv")] <- str_split_fixed(df$"1", ".21" , 2)
df[c("1", "indv")] <- str_split_fixed(df$"1", "Qc" , 2)
df <- df[ ,!names(df) %in% c("1", "2")]
df$treatment <- ifelse(grepl( "18", df$indv) | grepl( "19", df$indv) |  grepl( "26", df$indv) |  grepl( "30", df$indv) | grepl( "47", df$indv) | grepl( "52", df$indv), "drought", "control" )
return(df)
}) ##hago la modificacion en la fila de indv para que tenga el identificador del individuo


EW_20.21_feret$EW_2020_feret <-  merge(EW_20.21_feret$EW_2020_feret, Data_20.21$Data_2020, by= c("indv", "indv")) ##se unen los datos de ambos data frames
EW_20.21_feret$EW_2021_feret <-  merge(EW_20.21_feret$EW_2021_feret, Data_20.21$Data_2021, by= c("indv", "indv"))

EW_20.21_vul_index <- lapply(EW_20.21_feret, function(x){
  listas_xindv <-  split(x, x$indv)
  
  df <- lapply(listas_xindv, function(y){
    y$n_vessel <- nrow(y)
    y$vessel_frequency <- y$n/y$year_Total_area
    y$vul_index <- y$Dmean/y$vessel_frequency
    y$dh <- y$Sum.D5/y$Sum.D4
    y$Kh <- ((pi*998.2071)/(128*10^-3))*y$Sum.D4meter
    y <- data.frame("indv"= y$indv , "treatment"= y$treatment ,"vessel_frequency"= y$vessel_frequency, "vul_index"= y$vul_index, "dh"= y$dh, "kh" = y$Kh )
    y <- y[1,]
    return(y)
  })
  
  df <- do.call(rbind, Map(data.frame, df))
  return(df)
}) ##se calcula el EW vessel freq y el indice de vulnerabilidad



EW_20.21_vul_index <- rbind(EW_20.21_vul_index$EW_2020_feret, EW_20.21_vul_index$EW_2021_feret)

EW_20.21_vul_index$vul_indexNA <- ifelse(EW_20.21_vul_index$vul_index > 0.3, NA, EW_20.21_vul_index$vul_index)

shapiro.test(EW_20.21_vul_index$dh) ##es normal

ggplot()+ geom_boxplot(data= EW_20.21_vul_index, aes(x=as.factor(treatment), y= vessel_frequency))
ggplot()+ geom_boxplot(data= EW_20.21_vul_index, aes(x=as.factor(treatment), y= dh))
ggplot()+ geom_boxplot(data= EW_20.21_vul_index, aes(x=as.factor(treatment), y= kh))

  z <- 0
while(z < length(x)){
  z <- z +1
  x[[z]]$indv <- names(x[z])
} ###hace que nos cree la columna de individuos

df <- bind_rows(x) ###concentro todos los datos en un data frame

df[c("1", "2")] <-  str_split_fixed(df$indv, ".20" , 2)
df[c("1", "indv")] <- str_split_fixed(df$"1", ".21" , 2)
df[c("1", "indv")] <- str_split_fixed(df$"1", "Qc" , 2)
df <- df[ ,!names(df) %in% c("1", "2")]
df$treatment <- ifelse(grepl( "18", df$indv) | grepl( "19", df$indv) |  grepl( "26", df$indv) |  grepl( "30", df$indv) | grepl( "47", df$indv) | grepl( "52", df$indv), "drought", "control" )
return(df)
}) ##hago la modificacion en la fila de indv para que tenga el identificador del individuo


LW_20.21_feret$LW_2020_feret <-  merge(LW_20.21_feret$LW_2020_feret, Data_20.21$Data_2020, by= c("indv", "indv")) ##se unen los datos de ambos data frames
LW_20.21_feret$LW_2021_feret <-  merge(LW_20.21_feret$LW_2021_feret, Data_20.21$Data_2021, by= c("indv", "indv"))

LW_20.21_vul_index <- lapply(LW_20.21_feret, function(x){
  listas_xindv <-  split(x, x$indv)
  
  df <- lapply(listas_xindv, function(y){
    y$n_vessel <- nrow(y)
    y$vessel_frequency <- y$n/y$year_Total_area
    y$vul_index <- y$Dmean/y$vessel_frequency
    y$dh <- y$Sum.D5/y$Sum.D4
    y$Kh <- ((pi*998.2071)/(128*10^-3))*y$Sum.D4meter
    y <- data.frame("indv"= y$indv , "treatment"= y$treatment ,"vessel_frequency"= y$vessel_frequency, "vul_index"= y$vul_index, "dh" = y$dh, "kh" = y$Kh  )
    y <- y[1,]
    return(y)
  })
  
  df <- do.call(rbind, Map(data.frame, df))
  return(df)
}) ##se calcula el LW vessel freq y el indice de vulnerabilidad

return(x)}) 
x <- 0
while (x < length(area_2020)) {
  x <- x+1
  nombre <- names(area_2020[x])
  area_2020[[x]]$indv <- nombre
  if((names(area_2020[x]) == "g18QcS4F21.csv") | (names(area_2020[x]) == "h19QcS4F21.csv") | (names(area_2020[x]) == "i26QcS4F21.csv") | (names(area_2020[x]) == "m47QcS4F21.csv") | (names(area_2020[x]) == "n52QcS4F21.csv")){area_2020[[x]]$treatment <- "drought" }else{
    area_2020[[x]]$treatment <- "control"}
}
rm(x)
area_2020 <-  lapply(area_2020, function(x){x <- data.frame("Area"= x$area_total, "indv"= x$indv , "treatment" = x$treatment)
return(x)}) 

area_2020 <- do.call(rbind, Map(data.frame, area_2020))
area_2020 <-   area_2020[!duplicated(area_2020$Area), ]
area_2020$Area <- ifelse(area_2020$indv == "e9QcC4F21.csv",NA, area_2020$Area)

return(x)})

while (x < length(Area_2021)) {

  nombre <- names(Area_2021[x])
  Area_2021[[x]]$indv <- nombre
  if((names(Area_2021[x]) == "h18QcS19E22.csv") | (names(Area_2021[x]) == "i19QcS19E22.csv") | (names(Area_2021[x]) == "j30QcS19E22.csv") | (names(Area_2021[x]) == "o47QcS19E22.csv") | (names(Area_2021[x]) == "r52QcS19E22.csv")){Area_2021[[x]]$treatment <- "drought" }else{
    Area_2021[[x]]$treatment <- "control"}
}
rm(x)
Area_2021 <-  lapply(Area_2021, function(x){x <- data.frame("Area"= x$area_total, "indv"= x$indv , "treatment" = x$treatment)
return(x)}) 

Area_2021 <- do.call(rbind, Map(data.frame, Area_2021))
Area_2021 <-   Area_2021[!duplicated(Area_2021$Area), ]


Area_2021.20 <- rbind(Area_2021, area_2020)
```

```{r Relacion_crecimiento clima todo el periodo de crecimiento con TRW bruto, include=FALSE}
source("C:/Minicores/R_analisis/Analisis_anatomia/Anatomy_function.R") ##en windows
setwd("C:/Minicores/R_analisis")
###primero cremeamos hacemos una tabla con el crecimiento diario del arbol
Data2021_Bruto <- read_excel_allsheets("Datos_Modificados_Qca.xlsx")
Data2021_Bruto <- Data2021_Bruto[!names(Data2021_Bruto) %in% c("ray_area","10AGOS_FENO", "2Feb21")] ##elimino los elementos que no necesito
dates <- as.Date(c( "2021/03/18", "2021/04/01", "2021/04/15", "2021/04/21", "2021/06/10", "2021/07/22","2021/08/26", "2021/09/09", "2021/10/14", "2021/11/18", "2021/12/16","2022/01/19", "2022/02/8" )) ##creo un vector con las fecha
x <- 0
while(x < length(Data2021_Bruto)){ ##pongo una columna de fechas en cada elemento de la lista
  x <- x+1
  Data2021_Bruto[[x]]$date <- dates[x]
}
rm(x)

 Data2021_Bruto <- lapply(Data2021_Bruto, function(x){
  df <- x[!(is.na(x$Area) |x$Area == " ") ,  ] 
  df <- df[ ,names(df) %in% c("indv" ,"Plot","Tratam", "TRW (mm) arriba", "TRW (mm) centro", "TRW (mm) Abajo","date" ), drop = F]
  df$TRW_mean <- rowMeans(df[ ,names(df) %in% c("TRW (mm) arriba", "TRW (mm) centro", "TRW (mm) Abajo")], na.rm = T)
return(df)
}) ###me creo el TRW medio individual en cada fecha

###calculo el Z-score para estandarizar
 # z_score <- lapply(Data2021_Bruto, function(x){
 #   x <- x[complete.cases(x), ]
 #   x$TRW_mean <- as.numeric(x$TRW_mean)
 #   x$z_score <- (x$TRW_mean - mean(mean(x$TRW_mean, na.rm=T)))/ nrow(x)
 #   return(x)
 # })

# }) ###me creo un TRW medio de toda la fecha dependiendo del tratamiento
 
 
 ####hago el TRW estandarizado con log +1
 
#  Data2021_modelo <- do.call(rbind, Map(data.frame, Data2021_Bruto))
#  Data2021_modelo <- Data2021_modelo[complete.cases(Data2021_modelo), ]

#  shapiro.test(Data2021_modelogam$gam$residuals)
#  
#  pred_gamm4 <- get_predictions(Data2021_modelogam$gam, 
#                        cond = list(date = as.numeric(rep(unique(Data2021_modelo$date), 2)),
#                                    DBH_.cm. = mean(Data2021_modelo$DBH_.cm.),
#                                   Tratam = c("Control", "Sequía"),
#                                   se=T))
# 
# pred_gamm4$date <- as.Date(pred_gamm4$date)
 
 # ggplot()+
 #   geom_line(data = pred_gamm4, aes(x= date, fit, color= Tratam))+
 #   geom_point(data = Data2021_modelo, aes(x= date,TRWlog, color= Tratam))

  Data2021 <- lapply(Data2021_Bruto, function(x){
  df1 <- aggregate(TRW_mean~ Tratam, x , mean )
  df2 <- aggregate(TRW_mean~ Tratam,x, se )
  df <- cbind(df1, df2)
  names(df)[4] <- "TRW_se"
  df[3] <- NULL
  df$date <- x$date[1]
  return(df)
}) ###me creo un TRW medio de toda la fecha dependiendo del tratamiento

Data2021 <- do.call("rbind", Data2021) ####esto estaba en el codigo antiguo



###creo un crecimiento diario del arbol
# pred_gamm4$TRW_mean <- pred_gamm4$fit
# pred_gamm4 <- pred_gamm4[order(pred_gamm4$date),]
# Data2021 <- split(pred_gamm4, pred_gamm4$Tratam)

Data2021 <- split(Data2021, Data2021$Tratam)



Data2021 <- lapply(Data2021, function(x){ 
  z <- 0
  while(z < length(x$date)){
    z <- z+1
    ifelse(z == 1,x$difdate[[1]] <- 0.1, x$difdate[[z]] <- difftime(x$date[[z]], x$date[[z-1]], units = "days"))
  }
  x[is.na(x)] <- 0
  x$TRW_diff <- c(NA, diff(x$TRW_mean))
  x$TRW_diff[is.na(x$TRW_diff) | x$TRW_diff < 0 ] <- 0
  x$difdate <- as.numeric(x$difdate)
  x$TRW_daylyincrement <- x$TRW_diff/x$difdate
  return(x)
  }) ##esta funcion hace el incremento diario entre fechas


df_TRW_clima <- lapply(Data2021, function(x){
  difdate <- x$difdate ###me creo un vector para la diferencia entre fechas
  z <- 0
  df <- c()
  while(z < length(difdate)){ ##creo un vector con las repeticiones de la diferencia de fechas y en su contenido tiene el valor de TRW diario
    z <- z+1
    repeticiones <- rep(x$TRW_daylyincrement[z], times = difdate[z]) 
    df <- c(df, repeticiones)
  }
  df_TRW_clima <- data.frame("date" = seq.Date(from = as.Date("2021-03-19"), to= as.Date("2022-02-08"), by= "day"), "TRW_dayly" = df) ###se crea el data frame con las fechas de muestreo y a su vez el acumulado de crecimiento durante el estudio
  df_TRW_clima$TRW_dayly[1] <- df_TRW_clima$TRW_dayly[1] + x$TRW_mean[1] 
  df_TRW_clima$TRW_acum <- cumsum(df_TRW_clima$TRW_dayly)
  
    return(df_TRW_clima)
}) ##esta funcion me crea un nuevo data frame donde calculo el acumulado de TRW dia a dia

df_TRW_clima <- lapply(df_TRW_clima, function(x){
  Gamir_Nov21_clima_Feb22 <- Gamir_Nov21_Feb22_Temp[,!names(Gamir_Nov21_Feb22_Temp) %in% c("PET")]
  spei_data <- spei_data[,!names(spei_data) %in% c("sing")]
  names(spei_data)[1] <- "date" 
  
  result <- merge(x, Gamir_Nov21_clima_Feb22 , by= c("date", "date"))
  result <- merge(result, spei_data , by= c("date", "date"))
  result <- merge(result, HOBO_gamir , by= c("date", "date"))
  
  return(result)
}) ###uno ya el TRW acumualdo, con las variables climaticas


###creo las fechas anteriores de este incremento para hacer el los 30 dias antes del inicio de crecimiento
fecha_anterior <-  data.frame("date"= seq.Date(from = as.Date("2021-01-19"), to= as.Date("2021-03-18"), by= "day"))
Gamir_Nov21_Feb22_clima <- Gamir_Nov21_Feb22_Temp[,!names(Gamir_Nov21_Feb22_Temp) %in% c("PET")]
  spei_data <- spei_data[,!names(spei_data) %in% c("sing")]
  names(spei_data)[1] <- "date" 
fecha_anterior <- merge(fecha_anterior, Gamir_Nov21_Feb22_clima , by= c("date", "date"))
fecha_anterior <- merge(fecha_anterior, spei_data , by= c("date", "date"))
fecha_anterior <- merge(fecha_anterior, HOBO_gamir , by= c("date", "date"), all.x = TRUE)

fecha_anterior$TRW_dayly <- 0
fecha_anterior$TRW_acum <- 0

df_TRW_clima <- lapply(df_TRW_clima, function(x){
  df <- rbind(fecha_anterior, x)
  return(df)}) ###le añado el clima anterior


df_lag5 <- lapply(df_TRW_clima, function(x){
  TRW_acum <-  x[seq(from= 60, to =nrow(x) , by = 1), "TRW_acum"] ##con esto hago el acumulado desde el numero de fila 60, que se corresponde con la primera fecha de acumulado
  TRW_dayly <-  x[seq(from= 60, to =nrow(x) , by = 1), "TRW_dayly"]
  date <-  x[seq(from= 60, to =nrow(x) , by = 1), "date"]##con esto hago el cojo las fechas extradias desde el numero de fila 60, que se corresponde con la primera fecha de acumulado
   fechas <- c("date" = seq.Date(from = as.Date("2021-03-15"), to= as.Date("2022-02-08"), by= "day"))
   fechas <- x[x$date %in% fechas ,]
   prp <- fechas$prp
   temp_min <- fechas$Tmin
   temp_max <- fechas$Tmax
   spei <- fechas$spei
   VPD <- fechas$VPD

   prp_lag5 <- sum_every_n(prp,5)
   Tmin_lag5 <- mean_every_n(temp_min,5)
   Tmax_lag5 <- mean_every_n(temp_max,5)
   spei_lag5 <- mean_every_n(spei,5)
   VPD_lag5 <- mean_every_n(VPD,5)

  resultado <- data.frame("date" =date  ,"TRW_acum" =TRW_acum, "TRW_dayly" =TRW_dayly, "prp_Acum" = prp_lag5, "Tmin_mean" = Tmin_lag5,
                          "Tmax_mean"=Tmax_lag5, "spei_mean"=spei_lag5, "VPD"=VPD_lag5 )
  return(resultado)
}) ##funcion nueva, creo que es la mas indicada

df_lag10 <- lapply(df_TRW_clima, function(x){
  TRW_acum <-  x[seq(from= 60, to =nrow(x) , by = 1), "TRW_acum"] ##con esto hago el acumulado desde el numero de fila 60, que se corresponde con la primera fecha de acumulado
  date <-  x[seq(from= 60, to =nrow(x) , by = 1), "date"]##con esto hago el cojo las fechas extradias desde el numero de fila 60, que se corresponde con la primera fecha de acumulado
  TRW_dayly <-  x[seq(from= 60, to =nrow(x) , by = 1), "TRW_dayly"]
   fechas <- c("date" = seq.Date(from = as.Date("2021-03-10"), to= as.Date("2022-02-08"), by= "day"))
   fechas <- x[x$date %in% fechas ,]
   prp <- fechas$prp
   temp_min <- fechas$Tmin
   temp_max <- fechas$Tmax
   spei <- fechas$spei
   VPD <- fechas$VPD

   prp_lag10 <- sum_every_n(prp,10)
   Tmin_lag10 <- mean_every_n(temp_min,10)
   Tmax_lag10 <- mean_every_n(temp_max,10)
   spei_lag10 <- mean_every_n(spei,10)
   VPD_lag10 <- mean_every_n(VPD,10)

  resultado <- data.frame("date" =date  ,"TRW_acum" =TRW_acum,"TRW_dayly" =TRW_dayly, "prp_Acum" = prp_lag10, "Tmin_mean" = Tmin_lag10,
                          "Tmax_mean"=Tmax_lag10, "spei_mean"=spei_lag10, "VPD"=VPD_lag10 )
  return(resultado)
}) ##funcion nueva, creo que es la mas indicada

df_lag15 <- lapply(df_TRW_clima, function(x){
  TRW_acum <-  x[seq(from= 60, to =nrow(x) , by = 1), "TRW_acum"] ##con esto hago el acumulado desde el numero de fila 60, que se corresponde con la primera fecha de acumulado
  TRW_dayly <-  x[seq(from= 60, to =nrow(x) , by = 1), "TRW_dayly"]
  date <-  x[seq(from= 60, to =nrow(x) , by = 1), "date"]##con esto hago el cojo las fechas extradias desde el numero de fila 60, que se corresponde con la primera fecha de acumulado
   fechas <- c("date" = seq.Date(from = as.Date("2021-03-05"), to= as.Date("2022-02-08"), by= "day"))
   fechas <- x[x$date %in% fechas ,]
   prp <- fechas$prp
   temp_min <- fechas$Tmin
   temp_max <- fechas$Tmax
   spei <- fechas$spei
   VPD <- fechas$VPD

   prp_lag15 <- sum_every_n(prp,15)

   Tmax_lag15 <- mean_every_n(temp_max,15)
   spei_lag15 <- mean_every_n(spei,15)

                          "Tmax_mean"=Tmax_lag15, "spei_mean"=spei_lag15, "VPD"=VPD_lag15 )
  return(resultado)
}) ##funcion nueva, creo que es la mas indicada

df_lag30 <- lapply(df_TRW_clima, function(x){
  TRW_acum <-  x[seq(from= 60, to =nrow(x) , by = 1), "TRW_acum"] ##con esto hago el acumulado desde el numero de fila 60, que se corresponde con la primera fecha de acumulado
  TRW_dayly <-  x[seq(from= 60, to =nrow(x) , by = 1), "TRW_dayly"]
  date <-  x[seq(from= 60, to =nrow(x) , by = 1), "date"]##con esto hago el cojo las fechas extradias desde el numero de fila 60, que se corresponde con la primera fecha de acumulado
   fechas <- c("date" = seq.Date(from = as.Date("2021-02-18"), to= as.Date("2022-02-08"), by= "day"))
   fechas <- x[x$date %in% fechas ,]
   prp <- fechas$prp
   temp_min <- fechas$Tmin
   temp_max <- fechas$Tmax
   spei <- fechas$spei
   VPD <- fechas$VPD
   
   prp_lag30 <- sum_every_n(prp,30)
   Tmin_lag30 <- mean_every_n(temp_min,30)
   Tmax_lag30 <- mean_every_n(temp_max,30)
   spei_lag30 <- mean_every_n(spei,30)
   VPD_lag30 <- mean_every_n(VPD,30)
   
  resultado <- data.frame("date" =date  ,"TRW_acum" =TRW_acum, "TRW_dayly" = TRW_dayly , "prp_Acum" = prp_lag30, "Tmin_mean" = Tmin_lag30,
                          "Tmax_mean"=Tmax_lag30, "spei_mean"=spei_lag30, "VPD"=VPD_lag30 )
  return(resultado)
}) ##funcion nueva, creo que es la mas indicada

####voy a mirar si son normales las variables para ello hago el test de shapiro para todas las variables
clima <- df_TRW_clima$Control
shapiro.test(clima$Tmin) ###no tienen una distribucion normal
shapiro.test(clima$Tmax) ###no tienen una distribucion normal
shapiro.test(clima$prp) ###no tienen una distribucion normal
shapiro.test(clima$spei) ###no tienen una distribucion normal

###por lo tanto tengo que usar r de Spearman para calcular su correlacion

lista_lag <- list("lag5" =df_lag5, "lag10"= df_lag10, "lag15"= df_lag15,"lag30"= df_lag30)




resultados_correlacion_clima <- lapply(lista_lag, function(x){
  lapply(x, function(y){
   result_prp <- cor.test(y$TRW_dayly, y$prp_Acum , method = 'spearman', exact= FALSE)
   result_Tmin <- cor.test(y$TRW_dayly, y$Tmin_mean , method = 'spearman', exact= FALSE )
   result_Tmax <- cor.test(y$TRW_dayly, y$Tmax_mean , method = 'spearman', exact= FALSE )
   result_spei <- cor.test(y$TRW_dayly, y$spei_mean , method = 'spearman', exact= FALSE )
   result_VPD <- cor.test(y$TRW_dayly, y$VPD , method = 'spearman', exact= FALSE )
   print(result_prp)
   print(result_Tmin)
   print( result_Tmax)
   print(result_spei)
   print(result_VPD)
   resultado <- data.frame("prp" =result_prp$estimate, "Tmin" =result_Tmin$estimate, "Tmax"= result_Tmax$estimate, "spei" = result_spei$estimate, "VPD" = result_VPD$estimate)
   return(resultado)
   
   })}) ##spearman exact False ya que hay muchos empates

resultados_correlacion_clima <- lapply(resultados_correlacion_clima, function(x){
  resultado <- do.call(rbind,Map(data.frame, x) )
  resultado <- setDT(resultado, keep.rownames = "treatment")[]
 return(resultado)
})

nombres <- names(resultados_correlacion_clima)
x <- 0
while (x <length(resultados_correlacion_clima)) {
  x <- x+1
  resultados_correlacion_clima[[x]]$lag <- nombres[x]
}

resultados_correlacion_clima <- do.call(rbind,Map(data.frame, resultados_correlacion_clima) )
resultados_correlacion_clima$trat_lag <- as.factor(paste(resultados_correlacion_clima$treatment, resultados_correlacion_clima$lag, sep = "_"))


resultados_correlacion_clima$trat_lag <- fct_relevel(resultados_correlacion_clima$trat_lag,  "Control_lag5", "Control_lag10",  "Control_lag15",  "Control_lag30","Sequía_lag5", "Sequía_lag10","Sequía_lag15",  "Sequía_lag30" )

resultados_correlacion_clima$lag <- fct_relevel(resultados_correlacion_clima$lag,  "lag5","lag10", "lag15", "lag30" )


resultados_correlacion_clima_plot <- pivot_longer(resultados_correlacion_clima, cols=c("prp", "Tmin", "Tmax", "spei", "VPD"),
                    names_to='variable',
                    values_to='values')
resultados_correlacion_clima_plot$trat_lag <- as.factor(paste(resultados_correlacion_clima_plot$variable, resultados_correlacion_clima_plot$trat_lag, sep = "_"))

resultados_correlacion_clima_plot$trat_lag <- fct_relevel(resultados_correlacion_clima_plot$trat_lag,  "prp_Control_lag5","prp_Control_lag10", "prp_Control_lag15", "prp_Control_lag30", "prp_Sequía_lag5","prp_Sequía_lag10", "prp_Sequía_lag15", "prp_Sequía_lag30", "Tmin_Control_lag5","Tmin_Control_lag10", "Tmin_Control_lag15", "Tmin_Control_lag30", "Tmin_Sequía_lag5","Tmin_Sequía_lag10", "Tmin_Sequía_lag15", "Tmin_Sequía_lag30", "Tmax_Control_lag5", "Tmax_Control_lag10", "Tmax_Control_lag15", "Tmax_Control_lag30", "Tmax_Sequía_lag5", "Tmax_Sequía_lag10", "Tmax_Sequía_lag15", "Tmax_Sequía_lag30", "spei_Control_lag5", "spei_Control_lag10", "spei_Control_lag15", "spei_Control_lag30", "spei_Sequía_lag5", "spei_Sequía_lag10", "spei_Sequía_lag15", "spei_Sequía_lag30","VPD_Control_lag5","VPD_Control_lag10","VPD_Control_lag15","VPD_Control_lag30", "VPD_Sequía_lag5","VPD_Sequía_lag10","VPD_Sequía_lag15","VPD_Sequía_lag30", )

ggplot(resultados_correlacion_clima_plot, aes(x=as.factor(trat_lag), y= values))+
  geom_bar(aes(fill=lag), stat="identity")+
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = c(-0.5,-0.25,0,0.25,0.5), expand = c(-0.5,0.5))+
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#008B45","#00FF7F","#5CACEE","slateblue3"))+
  geom_hline(yintercept = c(-0.16,0.16), color="black", linetype= 2 )+
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color="black", linetype= 1 )+
  theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
          axis.line.x = element_line(colour="black"),
          axis.line.y = element_line(colour="black"),
        axis.text = element_text(color = "black",size = 12),
        axis.ticks.length  = unit(0.25, "cm"))+
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c(" "," ","Control"," "," "," ","Drought"," ", " "," ","Control"," "," "," ","Drought"," ", " "," ","Control"," "," "," ","Drought"," ", " "," ","Control"," "," "," ","Drought"," ", " "," ","Control"," "," "," ","Drought"," "  ))

df_TRW_clima_xplot <- do.call(rbind, Map(data.frame, df_TRW_clima))
df_TRW_clima_xplot <- setDT(df_TRW_clima_xplot, keep.rownames = "treatment")
df_TRW_clima_xplot$treatment <- ifelse(grepl( "Sequía", df_TRW_clima_xplot$treatment), "drought", "control" )


ggplot(df_TRW_clima_xplot, aes(x=date, y= TRW_dayly))+ geom_line(lwd=1)+
  theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
          axis.line.x = element_line(colour="black"),
          axis.line.y = element_line(colour="black"))+
  facet_grid(. ~ treatment)+
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 month" ,date_labels = "%m")
```

```{r Relacion_crecimiento clima por estaciones, include=FALSE}
##empiezo poniendole una nueva columna de estaciones
lista_lag <- lapply(lista_lag, function(x){
  nombres <- names(x)
  z <- 0
  while (z <length(x)) {
  z <- z+1
  x[[z]]$treatment <- nombres[z]
}
  resultado <- do.call(rbind,Map(data.frame, x) )
 return(resultado)
})

nombres <- names(lista_lag)
x <- 0
while (x <length(lista_lag)) {
  x <- x+1
  lista_lag[[x]]$lag <- nombres[x]
}
rm(x)
lista_lag <- do.call(rbind,Map(data.frame, lista_lag) )

seasons <- c("01" = "Winter", "02" = "Winter","03" = "Spring", "04" = "Spring", "05" = "Spring","06" = "Summer", "07" = "Summer", "08" = "Summer","09" = "Fall", "10" = "Fall", "11" = "Fall","12" = "Winter")
seasons <-seasons[format(lista_lag$date, "%m")]
lista_lag$season <- seasons

###separo el data frame dependiendo de la estacion
seasons <- split(lista_lag, lista_lag$season)


###empiezo a hacer el analisis con las estaciones

season_climate <- lapply(seasons, function(x){
  season <- split(x, x$lag)
  season <- lapply(season, function(z){split(z, z$treatment)  } )
  season_correlacion_clima <- lapply(season, function(x){
  lapply(x, function(y){
   result_prp <- cor.test(y$TRW_dayly, y$prp_Acum , method = 'spearman', exact= FALSE )
   result_Tmin <- cor.test(y$TRW_dayly, y$Tmin_mean , method = 'spearman', exact= FALSE )
   result_Tmax <- cor.test(y$TRW_dayly, y$Tmax_mean , method = 'spearman', exact= FALSE )
   result_spei <- cor.test(y$TRW_dayly, y$spei_mean , method = 'spearman', exact= FALSE )
   result_VPD <- cor.test(y$TRW_dayly, y$VPD , method = 'spearman', exact= FALSE )
   print(result_prp)
   print(result_Tmin)
   print( result_Tmax)
   print(result_spei)
   print(result_VPD)
   
   resultado <- data.frame("prp" =result_prp$estimate, "Tmin" =result_Tmin$estimate, "Tmax"= result_Tmax$estimate, "spei" = result_spei$estimate, "VPD" = result_VPD$estimate)
   return(resultado)
   
   })}) ##kendall ?¿

season_correlacion_clima <- lapply(season_correlacion_clima, function(x){
  resultado <- do.call(rbind,Map(data.frame, x) )
  resultado <- setDT(resultado, keep.rownames = "treatment")[]
 return(resultado)
})

nombres <- names(season_correlacion_clima)
x <- 0
while (x <length(season_correlacion_clima)) {
  x <- x+1
  season_correlacion_clima[[x]]$lag <- nombres[x]
}

season_correlacion_clima <- do.call(rbind,Map(data.frame, season_correlacion_clima) )
return(season_correlacion_clima)

}) ##aqui uno todo el codigo mediante listas

season_climate_plots <- lapply(season_climate, function(x){
  x$trat_lag <- as.factor(paste(x$treatment, x$lag, sep = "_"))

x$lag <- fct_relevel(x$lag,  "lag5","lag10", "lag15", "lag30" )


season_climate_plots <- pivot_longer(x, cols=c("prp", "Tmin", "Tmax", "spei", "VPD"),
                    names_to='variable',
                    values_to='values')
season_climate_plots$trat_lag <- as.factor(paste(season_climate_plots$variable, season_climate_plots$trat_lag, sep = "_"))

season_climate_plots$trat_lag <- fct_relevel(season_climate_plots$trat_lag,  "prp_Control_lag5","prp_Control_lag10", "prp_Control_lag15", "prp_Control_lag30", "prp_Sequía_lag5","prp_Sequía_lag10", "prp_Sequía_lag15", "prp_Sequía_lag30", "Tmin_Control_lag5","Tmin_Control_lag10", "Tmin_Control_lag15", "Tmin_Control_lag30", "Tmin_Sequía_lag5","Tmin_Sequía_lag10", "Tmin_Sequía_lag15", "Tmin_Sequía_lag30", "Tmax_Control_lag5", "Tmax_Control_lag10", "Tmax_Control_lag15", "Tmax_Control_lag30", "Tmax_Sequía_lag5", "Tmax_Sequía_lag10", "Tmax_Sequía_lag15", "Tmax_Sequía_lag30", "spei_Control_lag5", "spei_Control_lag10", "spei_Control_lag15", "spei_Control_lag30", "spei_Sequía_lag5", "spei_Sequía_lag10", "spei_Sequía_lag15", "spei_Sequía_lag30","VPD_Control_lag5","VPD_Control_lag10","VPD_Control_lag15","VPD_Control_lag30", "VPD_Sequía_lag5","VPD_Sequía_lag10","VPD_Sequía_lag15","VPD_Sequía_lag30", )

ggplot(season_climate_plots, aes(x=as.factor(trat_lag), y= values))+
  geom_bar(aes(fill=lag), stat="identity")+
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = c(-1,-0.75,-0.5,-0.25,0,0.25,0.5,0.75,1), limits = c(-1,1.1))+
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#008B45","#00FF7F","#5CACEE","slateblue3"))+
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color="black" )+
  geom_hline(yintercept = c(0.25,-0.25), color="black", linetype=2 )+
  theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
          axis.line.x = element_line(colour="black"),
          axis.line.y = element_line(colour="black"),
        axis.text = element_text(color = "black",size = 12),
        axis.ticks.length  = unit(0.25, "cm"))+
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c(" "," ","Control"," "," "," ","Drought"," ", " "," ","Control"," "," "," ","Drought"," ", " "," ","Control"," "," "," ","Drought"," ", " "," ","Control"," "," "," ","Drought"," ", " "," ","Control"," "," "," ","Drought"," "  ))
  
}) 

season_climate_plots$Fall
season_climate_plots$Spring
season_climate_plots$Summer
```

```{r Dendrometros, include=FALSE}
setwd("C:/Minicores/Dendrometros")
fechas_dendrometros <- c("28/01/21",	"04/02/21",	"18/02/21", "04/03/21"	,"18/03/21",	"01/04/21",	"15/04/21",	"22/04/21",	"29/04/21",	"13/05/21",	"28/05/21",	"10/06/21",	"24/06/21",	"08/07/21",	"22/07/21",	"05/08/21",	"19/08/21",	"26/08/21",	"09/09/21",	"17/09/21",	"23/09/21",	"14/10/21",	"27/10/21",	"18/11/21",	"30/11/21",	"16/12/21",	"20/12/21",	"19/01/22",	"28/01/22",	"24/02/22")
fechas_dendrometros <- as.Date(fechas_dendrometros, "%d/%m/%y")

Q.canariensis_dendrometro <- read_xlsx("Gamir_dendrometros.xlsx", sheet = "Qca")

Q.canariensis_dendrometro <- split(Q.canariensis_dendrometro, Q.canariensis_dendrometro$treatment)

Q.canariensis_dendrometro <-  lapply(Q.canariensis_dendrometro, function(x){
  x$indv <- as.factor(x$indv)
   media <- sapply(x, function(y)if(is.numeric(y)) mean(y, na.rm = TRUE))
   media <- media[!is.na(media)]
   media <- data.table(media)
   media$names <- names(x)
   media <- as.data.frame(media)
   
   media <- pivot_wider(media, names_from = "names", values_from = "media")
   
   columnas <- x[,! names(media) %in% c("treatment",  "indv")]
   columnas <- names(columnas)

     df <- pivot_longer(media, cols= columnas,
                    names_to='date',
                    values_to='increment')
     df$date <- fechas_dendrometros
     df$indv <- NULL
     df$treatment <- NULL
     
     df$increment <- as.numeric(df$increment)
     
     z <- 0
     while(z < length(df$date)){
    z <- z+1
    df$diffdate[z-1] <- difftime(df$date[z], df$date[z-1], units = "days")
     }
     
     df$TRW_daylyincrement <- df$increment/df$diffdate

     return(df)
  
}) ####no me funciona bien el comando diffdate y he tenido que hacer un "apaño" haciendo el crecimiento hasta la fecha

Q.canariensis_dendrometro_diario <- lapply(Q.canariensis_dendrometro, function(x) {
  x$TRW_daylyincrement[is.na(x$TRW_daylyincrement) | x$TRW_daylyincrement < 0 ] <- 0 
  
z <- 0
numero <- c()
while (z < (length(x$diffdate)-1)) {
  z <- z +1
  repeticionz <- rep(x$TRW_daylyincrement[z+1], times= x$diffdate[z])
  numero <- c(numero,repeticionz)
}
df_TRW_clima <- data.frame("date" = seq.Date(from = as.Date("2021-01-29"), to= as.Date("2022-02-24"), by= "day"), "TRW_dayly" = numero)
  
  return(df_TRW_clima)}) ####ahora calculo el incremento diario medido por dendrometros

Q.canariensis_dendrometro_clima <- lapply(Q.canariensis_dendrometro_diario, function(x){
  Gamir_Nov21_clima_Feb22 <- Gamir_Nov21_Feb22_Temp[,!names(Gamir_Nov21_Feb22_Temp) %in% c("PET")]
  spei_data <- spei_data[,!names(spei_data) %in% c("sing")]
  names(spei_data)[1] <- "date" 
  
  result <- merge(x, Gamir_Nov21_clima_Feb22 , by= c("date", "date"))
  result <- merge(result, spei_data , by= c("date", "date"))
  result <- merge(result, HOBO_gamir , by= c("date", "date"))
  
  return(result)
  
}) ###le añado el clima

df_lag5_Dendrometro <- lapply(Q.canariensis_dendrometro_clima, function(x){
  
  TRW_dayly <-  x[seq(from= 58, to =nrow(x) , by = 1), "TRW_dayly"]
  
  date <-  x[seq(from= 58, to =nrow(x) , by = 1), "date"]##con esto hago el cojo las fechas extradias desde el numero de fila 58, que se corresponde con la primera fecha de acumulado
   fechas <- c("date" = seq.Date(from = as.Date("2021-03-29"), to= as.Date("2022-02-24"), by= "day"))
   fechas <- x[x$date %in% fechas ,]
   prp <- fechas$prp
   temp_min <- fechas$Tmin
   temp_max <- fechas$Tmax
   spei <- fechas$spei
   VPD <- fechas$VPD

   prp_lag5 <- sum_every_n(prp,5)
   Tmin_lag5 <- mean_every_n(temp_min,5)
   Tmax_lag5 <- mean_every_n(temp_max,5)
   spei_lag5 <- mean_every_n(spei,5)
   VPD_lag5 <- mean_every_n(VPD,5)

  resultado <- data.frame("date" =date  , "TRW_dayly" =TRW_dayly, "prp_Acum" = prp_lag5, "Tmin_mean" = Tmin_lag5,
                          "Tmax_mean"=Tmax_lag5, "spei_mean"=spei_lag5, "VPD"=VPD_lag5 )
  return(resultado)
}) ##funcion nueva, creo que es la mas indicada

df_lag10_Dendrometro <- lapply(Q.canariensis_dendrometro_clima, function(x){
  TRW_dayly <-  x[seq(from= 58, to =nrow(x) , by = 1), "TRW_dayly"]
  date <-  x[seq(from= 58, to =nrow(x) , by = 1), "date"]##con esto hago el cojo las fechas extradias desde el numero de fila 58, que se corresponde con la primera fecha de acumulado
   fechas <- c("date" = seq.Date(from = as.Date("2021-03-24"), to= as.Date("2022-02-24"), by= "day"))
   fechas <- x[x$date %in% fechas ,]
   prp <- fechas$prp
   temp_min <- fechas$Tmin
   temp_max <- fechas$Tmax
   spei <- fechas$spei
   VPD <- fechas$VPD

   prp_lag10 <- sum_every_n(prp,10)
   Tmin_lag10 <- mean_every_n(temp_min,10)
   Tmax_lag10 <- mean_every_n(temp_max,10)
   spei_lag10 <- mean_every_n(spei,10)
   VPD_lag10 <- mean_every_n(VPD,10)

  resultado <- data.frame("date" =date  , "TRW_dayly" =TRW_dayly, "prp_Acum" = prp_lag10, "Tmin_mean" = Tmin_lag10,
                          "Tmax_mean"=Tmax_lag10, "spei_mean"=spei_lag10, "VPD"=VPD_lag10 )
  return(resultado)
  
}) ##funcion nueva, creo que es la mas indicada

   prp <- fechas$prp

df_lag30_Dendrometro <- lapply(Q.canariensis_dendrometro_clima, function(x){
  TRW_dayly <-  x[seq(from= 58, to =nrow(x) , by = 1), "TRW_dayly"]
  date <-  x[seq(from= 58, to =nrow(x) , by = 1), "date"]##con esto hago el cojo las fechas extradias desde el numero de fila 58, que se corresponde con la primera fecha de acumulado
   fechas <- c("date" = seq.Date(from = as.Date("2021-03-04"), to= as.Date("2022-02-24"), by= "day"))
   fechas <- x[x$date %in% fechas ,]
   prp <- fechas$prp

   Tmax_lag30 <- mean_every_n(temp_max,30)

                          "Tmax_mean"=Tmax_lag30, "spei_mean"=spei_lag30, "VPD"=VPD_lag30 )

}) ##funcion nueva, creo que es la mas indicada

lista_lag_dendrometro <- list("lag5" =df_lag5_Dendrometro, "lag10"= df_lag10_Dendrometro, "lag15"= df_lag15_Dendrometro,"lag30"= df_lag30_Dendrometro)

resultados_correlacion_clima_dendrometro <- lapply(lista_lag_dendrometro, function(x){
  lapply(x, function(y){
   result_prp <- cor.test(y$TRW_dayly, y$prp_Acum , method = 'kendall' )
   result_Tmin <- cor.test(y$TRW_dayly, y$Tmin_mean , method = 'kendall' )
   result_Tmax <- cor.test(y$TRW_dayly, y$Tmax_mean , method = 'kendall' )
   result_spei <- cor.test(y$TRW_dayly, y$spei_mean , method = 'kendall' )
   result_VPD <- cor.test(y$TRW_dayly, y$VPD , method = 'kendall' )
   
   resultado <- data.frame("prp" =result_prp$estimate, "Tmin" =result_Tmin$estimate, "Tmax"= result_Tmax$estimate, "spei" = result_spei$estimate, "VPD" = result_VPD$estimate)
   return(resultado)
   
   })}) ##kendall ?¿

resultados_correlacion_clima_dendrometro <- lapply(resultados_correlacion_clima_dendrometro, function(x){
  resultado <- do.call(rbind,Map(data.frame, x) )
  resultado <- setDT(resultado, keep.rownames = "treatment")[]
 return(resultado)
})

nombres <- names(resultados_correlacion_clima_dendrometro)
x <- 0
while (x <length(resultados_correlacion_clima_dendrometro)) {
  x <- x+1
  resultados_correlacion_clima_dendrometro[[x]]$lag <- nombres[x]
}

resultados_correlacion_clima_dendrometro <- do.call(rbind,Map(data.frame, resultados_correlacion_clima_dendrometro) )
resultados_correlacion_clima_dendrometro$trat_lag <- as.factor(paste(resultados_correlacion_clima_dendrometro$treatment, resultados_correlacion_clima_dendrometro$lag, sep = "_"))


resultados_correlacion_clima_dendrometro$trat_lag <- fct_relevel(resultados_correlacion_clima_dendrometro$trat_lag,  "Control_lag5", "Control_lag10",  "Control_lag15",  "Control_lag30","Sequía_lag5", "Sequía_lag10","Sequía_lag15",  "Sequía_lag30" )

resultados_correlacion_clima_dendrometro$lag <- fct_relevel(resultados_correlacion_clima_dendrometro$lag,  "lag5","lag10", "lag15", "lag30" )


resultados_correlacion_clima_dendrometro <- pivot_longer(resultados_correlacion_clima_dendrometro, cols=c("prp", "Tmin", "Tmax", "spei", "VPD"),
                    names_to='variable',
                    values_to='values')
resultados_correlacion_clima_dendrometro$trat_lag <- as.factor(paste(resultados_correlacion_clima_dendrometro$variable, resultados_correlacion_clima_dendrometro$trat_lag, sep = "_"))

resultados_correlacion_clima_dendrometro$trat_lag <- fct_relevel(resultados_correlacion_clima_dendrometro$trat_lag,  "prp_Control_lag5","prp_Control_lag10", "prp_Control_lag15", "prp_Control_lag30", "prp_Sequía_lag5","prp_Sequía_lag10", "prp_Sequía_lag15", "prp_Sequía_lag30", "Tmin_Control_lag5","Tmin_Control_lag10", "Tmin_Control_lag15", "Tmin_Control_lag30", "Tmin_Sequía_lag5","Tmin_Sequía_lag10", "Tmin_Sequía_lag15", "Tmin_Sequía_lag30", "Tmax_Control_lag5", "Tmax_Control_lag10", "Tmax_Control_lag15", "Tmax_Control_lag30", "Tmax_Sequía_lag5", "Tmax_Sequía_lag10", "Tmax_Sequía_lag15", "Tmax_Sequía_lag30", "spei_Control_lag5", "spei_Control_lag10", "spei_Control_lag15", "spei_Control_lag30", "spei_Sequía_lag5", "spei_Sequía_lag10", "spei_Sequía_lag15", "spei_Sequía_lag30","VPD_Control_lag5","VPD_Control_lag10","VPD_Control_lag15","VPD_Control_lag30", "VPD_Sequía_lag5","VPD_Sequía_lag10","VPD_Sequía_lag15","VPD_Sequía_lag30", )

resultados_correlacion_clima_dendrometro_plot <- ggplot(resultados_correlacion_clima_dendrometro, aes(x=as.factor(trat_lag), y= values))+
  geom_bar(aes(fill=lag), stat="identity")+
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = c(-0.5,-0.25,0,0.25,0.5), expand = c(-0.5,0.5))+
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#008B45","#00FF7F","#5CACEE","slateblue3"))+
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color="black" )+
  theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
          axis.line.x = element_line(colour="black"),
          axis.line.y = element_line(colour="black"),
        axis.text = element_text(color = "black",size = 12),
        axis.ticks.length  = unit(0.25, "cm"))+
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c(" "," ","Control"," "," "," ","Drought"," ", " "," ","Control"," "," "," ","Drought"," ", " "," ","Control"," "," "," ","Drought"," ", " "," ","Control"," "," "," ","Drought"," ", " "," ","Control"," "," "," ","Drought"," "  ))
resultados_correlacion_clima_dendrometro_plot

df_Dendrometro_dyaly_plot <- do.call(rbind, Map(data.frame, Q.canariensis_dendrometro))
df_Dendrometro_dyaly_plot <- setDT(df_Dendrometro_dyaly_plot, keep.rownames = "treatment")
df_Dendrometro_dyaly_plot$treatment <- ifelse(grepl( "Sequía", df_Dendrometro_dyaly_plot$treatment), "drought", "control" )
ggplot(df_Dendrometro_dyaly_plot, aes(x=date, y= TRW_daylyincrement))+ geom_line(lwd=1)+
  theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
          axis.line.x = element_line(colour="black"),
          axis.line.y = element_line(colour="black"),
        axis.text = element_text(color = "black",size = 12),
        axis.ticks.length  = unit(0.25, "cm"))+
  facet_grid(. ~ treatment)+
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 month" ,date_labels = "%m")+
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0)

```

```{r Diferencias TRW, include=FALSE}
###cojo los datos de TRW individual de cada fecha con el medio de cada fecha

TRW_2021 <- lapply(Data2021_Bruto, function(x) {x <-  x[!(names(x) %in% c("TRW (mm) Abajo" ,"TRW (mm) centro" , "TRW (mm) arriba"))]
x <- na.omit(x)
return(x)}) ##cojo solo el TRW medio individual


TRW_2021 <- do.call(rbind, Map(data.frame, TRW_2021)) ####lo uno todo

# cargo los datos de patogenicidad
patogenos <- read_xlsx("C:/Minicores/Patogenos/Patogenos y textura.xlsx", sheet = "Gamir")

patogenos2020 <- data.frame("indv" = patogenos$Tree, "Pci"= patogenos$media)

TRW_patogeno <- merge(TRW_2021,patogenos2020, by=c("indv","indv"))

names(DBH_H)[names(DBH_H) == "Árbol" ] <- "indv"  ##este data frame lo he cogido de los analisis 2020/2021

DBH_H <- DBH_H[!(names(DBH_H) %in% c("Tratam" ,"Plot" , "Finca","Especie"))]

TRW_patogeno_DBH_H <- merge(TRW_patogeno,DBH_H, by=c("indv","indv")) ###uno los datos del arbol, de patogenos y TRW

####voy a hacer el analisis para la tabla de patogenos

patogenos <- split(patogenos, patogenos$Species)
patogenos <- patogenos$Quejigo

####usar la distribucion binomial negativa  usando glmmTMB para comparar


anv_patogenos <- glmmTMB( Pci2020 ~ Drought,data = patogenos, family = nbinom2())
summary(anv_patogenos)

ggplot(data = patogenos, aes(x=Drought, y=Pci2020))+ geom_point()

aggregate(Pci2020 ~ Drought, patogenos, mean )
aggregate(Pci2020 ~ Drought, patogenos, se )



###ahora hago los analisis de tendencias
ggplot(data = TRW_patogeno_DBH_H, aes(x=date, y=TRW_mean, color=Tratam))+geom_point()+
  scale_color_manual(values =c("darkslategray3","#FF4040"))

cor(TRW_patogeno_DBH_H[,5:9], method = "pearson")

TRW_patogeno_DBH_H$indv <- as.factor(TRW_patogeno_DBH_H$indv)
TRW_patogeno_DBH_H$Plot <- as.factor(TRW_patogeno_DBH_H$Plot)
TRW_patogeno_DBH_H$date1 <- as.numeric(TRW_patogeno_DBH_H$date) ###lo combierto en numerico ya que no funciona como fecha  el modelo, pero lo guardo en otra columna4
###modelos

shapiro.test(TRW_patogeno_DBH_H$TRW_mean) ###no es normal, poor lo tanto cambio los datos a normales
skewness(TRW_patogeno_DBH_H$TRW_mean, na.rm = TRUE) #veo que dispersion tiene los datos
TRW_patogeno_DBH_H$TRW_trans <- log10(TRW_patogeno_DBH_H$TRW_mean +1) ##realizo la transformacion
qqnorm(TRW_patogeno_DBH_H$TRW_trans)
qqline(TRW_patogeno_DBH_H$TRW_trans)
shapiro.test(EW20.21$trans) ###veo que mis datos ya son normales
hist(EW20.21$trans)


###no utilizo la H porque esta correlacionada con el DBH
TRW_patogeno_DBH_H$DBH_.cm. <- TRW_patogeno_DBH_H$`DBH_(cm)`
TRW_patogeno_DBH_H$Diametro_copa_.cm. <- TRW_patogeno_DBH_H$`Diametro_copa_(cm)`


modelo1 <- gamm4(TRW_trans ~ s(date1)+s(Pci) + Tratam + s(DBH_.cm.) + s(Diametro_copa_.cm.),data = TRW_patogeno_DBH_H, random=~(1|Plot/indv))

modelo2 <- gamm4(TRW_trans ~ s(date1)+s(Pci) + Tratam + s(DBH_.cm.) ,data = TRW_patogeno_DBH_H, random=~(1|Plot/indv))

modelo3 <- gamm4(TRW_trans ~ s(date1)+s(Pci) + Tratam + s(Diametro_copa_.cm.),data = TRW_patogeno_DBH_H, random=~(1|Plot/indv))


modelo4 <- gamm4(TRW_trans ~ s(date1)+s(Pci) + s(DBH_.cm.) + s(Diametro_copa_.cm.),data = TRW_patogeno_DBH_H, random=~(1|Plot/indv))

modelo5 <- gamm4(TRW_trans ~ s(date1)+s(Pci) + s(Diametro_copa_.cm.),data = TRW_patogeno_DBH_H, random=~(1|Plot/indv))

modelo6 <- gamm4(TRW_trans ~ s(date1)+s(Pci) + s(DBH_.cm.) ,data = TRW_patogeno_DBH_H, random=~(1|Plot/indv))

modelo8 <- gamm4(TRW_trans ~ s(date1) + Tratam + s(DBH_.cm.) + s(Diametro_copa_.cm.),data = TRW_patogeno_DBH_H, random=~(1|Plot/indv))

modelo9 <- gamm4(TRW_trans ~ s(date1) + s(DBH_.cm.) + s(Diametro_copa_.cm.),data = TRW_patogeno_DBH_H, random=~(1|Plot/indv))

modelo10 <- gamm4(TRW_trans ~ s(date1) + s(Diametro_copa_.cm.),data = TRW_patogeno_DBH_H, random=~(1|Plot/indv))

modelo11 <- gamm4(TRW_trans ~ s(date1) + Tratam,data = TRW_patogeno_DBH_H, random=~(1|Plot/indv))

modelo12 <- gamm4(TRW_trans ~ s(date1)+s(Pci) + Tratam ,data = TRW_patogeno_DBH_H, random=~(1|Plot/indv))

modelo7 <- gamm4(TRW_trans ~ s(date1)+s(Pci) ,data = TRW_patogeno_DBH_H, random=~(1|Plot/indv))

modelo13 <- gamm4(TRW_trans ~ s(date1)+s(DBH_.cm.) ,data = TRW_patogeno_DBH_H, random=~(1|Plot/indv))

model.sel(modelo1$mer, modelo2$mer, modelo3$mer, modelo4$mer, modelo5$mer,modelo7$mer, modelo8$mer, modelo9$mer, modelo10$mer, modelo11$mer,modelo12$mer, modelo13$mer) ###los mas significativos es el modelo 11 con -287.1 de AICc
summary(modelo1$gam)
sum(summary(modelo1$gam)$s.table[, "edf"])
summary(modelo2$gam)
sum(summary(modelo2$gam)$s.table[, "edf"])
summary(modelo3$gam)
sum(summary(modelo3$gam)$s.table[, "edf"])
summary(modelo4$gam)
sum(summary(modelo4$gam)$s.table[, "edf"])
summary(modelo5$gam)
sum(summary(modelo5$gam)$s.table[, "edf"])
summary(modelo6$gam)
sum(summary(modelo6$gam)$s.table[, "edf"])
summary(modelo7$gam)
sum(summary(modelo7$gam)$s.table[, "edf"])
summary(modelo8$gam)
sum(summary(modelo8$gam)$s.table[, "edf"])
summary(modelo9$gam)
sum(summary(modelo9$gam)$s.table[, "edf"])
summary(modelo10$gam)
sum(summary(modelo10$gam)$s.table[, "edf"])

summary(modelo12$gam)
sum(summary(modelo12$gam)$s.table[, "edf"])

sum(summary(modelo13$gam)$s.table[, "edf"])

###veo la normalidad del modelo
residuos <- residuals(modelo11$mer)
qqnorm(residuos)
qqline(residuos)
hist(residuos) ###son normales

#### hay significaciancia de crecimiento menor en control

pred1 <- get_predictions(modelo11$gam, 
                       cond = list(date1 = seq(min(TRW_patogeno_DBH_H$date1, na.rm=T),
                                            max(TRW_patogeno_DBH_H$date1, na.rm=T), 
                                            length.out = nrow(TRW_patogeno_DBH_H )),
                                  Tratam = c("Control", "Sequía"),
                                  se=T))

pred1$date <- as.Date(pred1$date1)

ggplot(data=pred1)+
  geom_line(mapping = aes(x=date, y=fit, color= Tratam), show.legend = F)+
  geom_ribbon(alpha=.4,mapping = aes(x=date, ymin=fit-CI, ymax=fit+CI, fill=Tratam), show.legend = F)+
  geom_point(data = TRW_patogeno_DBH_H, mapping =  aes(x=date, y=as.numeric(TRW_trans), color=Tratam), show.legend = F)+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#1E90FF","#FF4040"))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#97FFFF","#FF6A6A"))+
  theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
          axis.line.x = element_line(colour="black"),
          axis.line.y = element_line(colour="black"),
        axis.text = element_text(color = "black",size = 12),
        axis.ticks.length  = unit(0.1, "cm"))


####ahora lo intento con el TRW diario
dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H <- split(TRW_patogeno_DBH_H, TRW_patogeno_DBH_H$indv)


dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H <- lapply(dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H, function(x){x <-  x[order(as.Date(x$date, format="%y/%m/%d")),]
diff <- diff(x$TRW_mean)
diff <- c(0, diff)
diff <- ifelse(diff < 0, 0, diff)
x$diff <- diff
ndays <- diff(x$date1)
ndays <- c(0, ndays)
x$days <- ndays
x$TRW_day <- diff/ndays
x[is.na(x)] <- 0
return(x)}) ##ordeno las fechas hago la resta de una menos la otra

dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H <- do.call(rbind, Map(data.frame, dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H))
# 
# dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$diff <- ifelse(dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$diff > 1, 0.7, dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$diff)
# dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$diff <- ifelse(dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$date1 == 19031 , 0 , dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$diff) ##medidas de febrero no son muy buenas
# dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$diff <- ifelse((dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$date == 19011) & (dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$diff > 0.88) , 0 , dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$diff) ##para este individuo cogí una zona de elongacion
# dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$diff_mod <- ifelse(dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$diff == 0, NA, dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$diff)




shapiro.test(dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$TRW_day) ##no son normales estos datos, por los tanto, los transformo
hist(dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$TRW_day)

skewness(dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$TRW_day, na.rm = TRUE) #veo que dispersion tiene los datos
dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$diff_trans <- log10(dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$TRW_day +1) ##realizo la transformacion

 
dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$diff_trans <- ifelse(dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$date1 ==19031, 0, dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$diff_trans  ) ###he cogido algo de zonas de elongacion al final de crecimiento
dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$diff_trans <- ifelse(dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$date1 == 18977 & dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$indv ==47, 0.003 , dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$diff_trans  ) 

# dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$diff_trans_sin0 <- ifelse(dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$diff_trans == 0, NA, dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$diff_trans)
# qqnorm(dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$diff_trans_sin0)
# qqline(dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$diff_trans_sin0)
# shapiro.test(dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$diff_trans_sin0) ###veo que mis datos han mejorado la normalidad
# hist(dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$diff_trans_sin0)


modelo1 <- gamm4(diff_trans ~ s(date1) + Tratam, data = dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H, random=~(1|Plot/indv))
modelo2 <- gamm4(diff_trans ~ s(date1) + Tratam + s(Pci) , data = dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H, random=~(1|Plot/indv))
modelo3 <- gamm4(diff_trans ~ s(date1) + Tratam + s(Pci) + s(DBH_.cm.) + s(Diametro_copa_.cm.), data = dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H, random=~(1|Plot/indv))
modelo4 <- gamm4(diff_trans ~ s(date1) + s(Pci) + s(DBH_.cm.) + s(Diametro_copa_.cm.), data = dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H, random=~(1|Plot/indv))

model.sel(modelo1$mer, modelo2$mer, modelo3$mer,modelo4$mer) ###se usa el modelo 1, ya que es el que posee menor AICc
###veo la normalidad del modelo
residuos <- residuals(modelo1$mer)
qqnorm(residuos)
qqline(residuos)
hist(residuos) ###son normales

modelo1 <- gamm4(diff_trans ~ s(date1) + Tratam, data = dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H, random=~(1|Plot/indv))


pred_TRWday <- get_predictions(modelo1$gam, 
                       cond = list(date1 = seq(min(dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$date1, na.rm=T),
                                            max(dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H$date1, na.rm=T), 
                                            length.out = nrow(dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H )),
                                  Tratam = c("Control", "Sequía"),
                                  se=T))

pred_TRWday$date <- as.Date(pred_TRWday$date1)

TRW_dayly_Plot <- ggplot(data=pred_TRWday)+
  geom_line(mapping = aes(x=date, y=fit, color= Tratam), show.legend = F)+
  geom_ribbon(alpha=.4,mapping = aes(x=date, ymin=fit-CI, ymax=fit+CI, fill=Tratam), show.legend = F)+
  geom_point(data = dayTRW_patogeno_DBH_H, mapping =  aes(x=date, y=as.numeric(diff_trans), color=Tratam), show.legend = F)+
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#1E90FF","#FF4040"))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#97FFFF","#FF6A6A"))+
  theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
          axis.line.x = element_line(colour="black"),
          axis.line.y = element_line(colour="black"),
        axis.text = element_text(color = "black",size = 12),
        axis.ticks.length  = unit(0.1, "cm"))+
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0)+
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-0.002,0.025))+
  ylab("log(TRW(mm/day) +1)")

library(plotly)
ggplotly(TRW_dayly_Plot)

```

```{r Todos los Cortes_Qca, include=FALSE}
####1-ABRIL-2021
D1Abr21 <-read_excel("C:/Minicores/R_analisis/Datos_Modificados_Qca.xlsx", 
    sheet = "1Abr2021") ###abro el archivo
D1Abr21 <- D1Abr21[!is.na(D1Abr21$Codigo),] ###elimino los individuos que no se han medido
D1Abr21 <- D1Abr21[!(D1Abr21$N_vasos <= 0),] ###elimino los individuos que se han medido pero no hay crecimiento anual


names <- D1Abr21$indv
path <-  "C:/Minicores/Qca_Bruto/ImageJ/Tablas/c1-4-2021"
setwd(path)
name_documents <- list.files(pattern = '*.csv')
c1A21 <- lapply(name_documents, read_delim, delim = ',')

names(c1A21) <- names
c1A21 <- lapply(c1A21, function(x) Feret_converter_analisistemporal(x,"mm"))

c1A21 <- bind_rows(c1A21) 
rownames(c1A21) <- names 
c1A21$indv <- names
D1Abr21 <- mutate(D1Abr21, TRW_mean = rowMeans(select(D1Abr21, 
                                              c("TRW (mm) arriba","TRW (mm) centro","TRW (mm) Abajo")), na.rm = TRUE))
D1Abr21 <- select_columns(D1Abr21)
c1A21 <- merge(c1A21, D1Abr21, by= c("indv", "indv"))
c1A21 <- Vessel_measurement(c1A21)
```
